# Why Kubernetes Doesn't Restart Pods on ConfigMap Change

The kubelet propagates mounted ConfigMap and Secret content to disk
eventually, but it never signals the consumer process. Recurring class of
bug across charts and homegrown manifests.

> Prescriptive strategy ranking (Reloader preferred, Helm checksum, manual
> last) lives in
> [standards/kubernetes/configmap-reload.md](https://github.com/jewzaam/standards/blob/main/kubernetes/configmap-reload.md).

## The default behavior

Kubernetes does not restart Pods when the content of a mounted ConfigMap or
Secret changes. The mounted file on disk updates (eventually, via kubelet
sync), but processes that read the file once at startup keep the old
content in memory until the Pod restarts for some other reason.

Do not assume "GitOps applied my change, therefore the app sees it." Pick a
reload strategy explicitly per workload — the kubelet does not give you one
for free.

## Reloader's annotation watches direct mounts only

[stakater/Reloader](https://github.com/stakater/Reloader)'s
`reloader.stakater.com/auto: "true"` only watches CMs and Secrets that the
Pod mounts **directly** (via `volumes:` referencing the resource by name).

ConfigMaps that reach a Pod **indirectly** are invisible to auto-detect.
Example: grafana's `sc-datasources` sidecar watches the cluster for
ConfigMaps with a label, reads them, and writes the merged result into a
shared `emptyDir` volume. The grafana container only mounts the emptyDir —
it never mounts the source ConfigMap. Reloader sees no link.

For indirect references, name the ConfigMap explicitly:

```yaml
metadata:
  annotations:
    configmap.reloader.stakater.com/reload: "<cm-name>,<other-cm-name>"
```

The same pattern works for Secrets via
`secret.reloader.stakater.com/reload`.

## Helm `checksum/config` covers only what the chart author hashes

Many community charts embed:

```yaml
spec:
  template:
    metadata:
      annotations:
        checksum/config: {{ include (print $.Template.BasePath "/configmap.yaml") . | sha256sum }}
```

Any change to the ConfigMap content changes the checksum, which changes the
Pod template hash, which forces a rollout. Works without an extra controller
but only covers ConfigMaps the chart author opted to hash. New ConfigMaps
added by an end-user values override are not covered.
